title: Fixing CSS Grid Layout with Inconsistent Column Width Calculation and Variable Item Count

statement.markdown: Given a grid layout with `grid-template-columns` defined as `repeat(4, 1fr)`, implement a function to calculate the width of each column in pixels. The input will be an array of integers representing the number of items in each column. The function should return an array of integers representing the width of each column in pixels. Note that the total width of the grid is 800 pixels.
statement.inputFormat: An array of integers representing the number of items in each column
statement.outputFormat: An array of integers representing the width of each column in pixels
statement.notes: The input array will not be empty and the total number of items across all columns will not exceed 16.

examples.explanation: The total width of the grid is 800 pixels, and the number of items in each column is 3, 2, 1, and 4 respectively. The width of each column should be calculated as 200, 400, 400, and 100 pixels respectively.
The total width of the grid is 800 pixels, and the number of items in each column is 5. The width of each column should be calculated as 200 pixels.

hints.text: Consider the total number of items across all columns to calculate the width of each column.

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.intuition: The key to solving this problem is to calculate the total number of items across all columns and then divide the total width of the grid by the total number of items to get the width of each column.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would involve calculating the width of each column individually, which would result in a time complexity of O(n).
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves calculating the total number of items across all columns and then dividing the total width of the grid by the total number of items to get the width of each column. This approach results in a time complexity of O(1).
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness can be shown by considering the total width of the grid and the number of items in each column. The width of each column should be calculated as the total width of the grid divided by the total number of items across all columns.
